facts_disclosed: Nami typeface abandoned — too much work for no pay

turns.content: you know what, i need to say this out loud to make it real. the nami typeface is dead. i'm done with it
That sounds like a big decision. What's the typeface — and what brought you to this point?

it was this hybrid japanese-english font i've been working on as a side project. the whole concept was that the kanji and latin characters would share the same visual weight so they'd look cohesive together. honestly it was a cool idea and i was really into it for a while but i'm killing it. it's not happening

That does sound like a genuinely interesting concept — getting kanji and Latin characters to share visual weight is a real typographic challenge. What's making you pull the plug? Is it a technical wall, or something else?

it's scope honestly. i sat down last week and actually mapped out what it would take to finish. like properly finish, with all the kanji coverage you'd need for it to be usable. we're talking two years minimum of nights and weekends. two years. for something that realistically nobody is going to pay for

Two years is a huge commitment for a side project, especially one without a clear revenue path. The independent typeface market is genuinely difficult — even well-known type designers struggle to make money from individual releases unless they have an existing audience or a foundry behind them. Is it purely the economics, or is there also a motivation issue?

both. like even if i could sell it, independent typefaces barely move anymore. everyone either uses google fonts or they license from the big foundries. a solo designer releasing a niche hybrid font? honestly who's buying that. and yeah the motivation has been dying for a while. i keep opening the files and just staring at them and then closing them and doing client work instead

The staring-and-closing cycle is usually a pretty clear signal. When a project stops pulling you in and starts feeling like an obligation, the energy it takes to keep it alive often costs more than it's worth — especially when you have paying work competing for the same hours.

Is there any version of this that's smaller in scope that might still be worthwhile? Like a limited character set or just the Latin half?

no. honestly that was my first thought too, like maybe i scale it down. but the whole point of nami was the hybrid approach. if i strip that out it's just another latin sans-serif and the world doesn't need another one of those. so it's all or nothing and i'm choosing nothing. forget i ever mentioned it. it was a fantasy

That's fair — if the concept only works at full scope and full scope isn't realistic, then scaling down would just produce something you're not excited about anyway. There's no shame in killing a project when the math doesn't work. Two years of unpaid nights and weekends is a real cost, not just an abstract one.

Does letting it go feel like relief, or does it sting?

honestly a bit of both. like it stings because i genuinely loved the concept and i had some really nice letterforms sketched out. but the relief is bigger. i've been carrying this guilt about not working on it for months and now i can just... stop feeling guilty about it. i need to focus on work that actually pays. the freelance thing is stressful enough without adding a two-year charity project on top

The guilt removal alone is probably worth the decision. Carrying around a project you're not working on but feel like you should be is genuinely draining — it's this low-level background anxiety that eats into your energy even when you're doing other things.

And focusing on paid work when freelancing is feast-or-famine isn't giving up on creativity, it's just being realistic about what you need right now. The skills and ideas you developed working on it don't disappear — they'll show up in other work.
yeah. yeah that's true. okay it's officially dead. nami is dead. i'm going to delete the project folder so i don't torture myself with it. onto things that actually keep the lights on

Deleting the folder is a strong move — clean break. If you want a compromise, you could archive it somewhere you won't stumble across it, but honestly if you know yourself and you know you'll keep reopening it out of guilt, deleting might be the healthier call.

Go focus on the paying work. You made the right call.

yeah no i need the clean break. if the files are there i'll just keep second-guessing myself. nami is done, it's abandoned, i'm not going back to it. thanks for letting me say it out loud, that actually helped
Sometimes you just need to declare it to make it stick. The Nami typeface had a good concept behind it, but killing it is the right call given where you are. No guilt, no going back. Go get paid.
